message:
feat: LISR-1 Security Contract and Repair Policy Framework (bd-k5q5.9.1)

- RepairMode enum (Off/Suggest/AutoSafe/AutoStrict) replaces boolean flag
- Repair mode gated at module resolution level (static-only safety boundary)
- Privilege monotonicity checker blocks repairs that escape extension root
- RepairRisk classification gates aggressive patterns behind AutoStrict
- 108 tests covering all repair infrastructure

Closes: bd-k5q5.9.1.1, bd-k5q5.9.1.2, bd-k5q5.9.1.3, bd-k5q5.9.1.4, bd-k5q5.9.1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -738,11 +738,11 @@
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","title":"Integration tests: verify auto-repair with real extensions","description":"## What

After all 5 patterns are implemented, create an integration test suite that
verifies auto-repair works end-to-end with real extension artifacts.  The test
should:

1. Remove the manual stubs we created (shared/index.ts, dist/extension.js,
   form/*.html, constants.ts, utils.ts, processes-component.ts)
2. Load the previously-broken extensions
3. Verify they load successfully via auto-repair
4. Verify the correct repair events are emitted
5. Verify the extensions register the expected commands/tools/hooks

## Test Structure

\`tests/extension_auto_repair.rs\`:

```rust
#[test]
fn auto_repair_dist_to_src_fallback() {
    // Remove dist/extension.js stub for community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip
    // Load the extension
    // Verify it loads and registers expected items
    // Verify RepairEvent(DistToSrc) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_missing_asset() {
    // Remove form/ stubs for nicobailon-interview-tool
    // Load the extension
    // Verify it loads (server.ts gets empty HTML/CSS/JS)
    // Verify RepairEvent(MissingAsset) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_monorepo_escape() {
    // Remove shared/index.ts stub
    // Load qualisero-background-notify
    // Verify it loads with auto-generated stub
    // Verify RepairEvent(MonorepoEscape) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_proxy_stub() {
    // Remove a hand-written virtual module
    // Load extension that depends on it
    // Verify it loads via proxy stub
    // Verify RepairEvent(MissingNpmDep) was emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_disabled() {
    // Set auto_repair_enabled = false
    // Try loading a broken extension
    // Verify it fails with the original error
    // Verify no repair events emitted
}

#[test]
fn auto_repair_never_stubs_system_modules() {
    // Verify that node:fs, child_process, etc. are never proxy-stubbed
    // Even when auto_repair is enabled
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] At least 6 integration tests covering all 5 patterns + disabled mode
- [ ] Tests use real extension artifacts (not synthetic)
- [ ] Tests verify both success (extension loads) and observability (events emitted)
- [ ] Tests verify the kill-switch works (auto_repair_enabled = false)
- [ ] All tests pass in CI

## Why This Matters

The integration tests are our proof that auto-repair works correctly and safely.
They're also the regression safety net — if a future change breaks auto-repair,
these tests catch it.

## Estimated Effort: 3-4 hours

## Files to Create
- tests/extension_auto_repair.rs

## Dependencies
- Requires ALL 5 patterns to be implemented
- Requires bd-k5q5.8.1 (logging), bd-k5q5.8.2 (P1), bd-k5q5.8.3 (P2),
  bd-k5q5.8.4 (P3), bd-k5q5.8.5 (P4), bd-k5q5.8.6 (P5)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:14.532673214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:37:08.298334621Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auto-repair","extensions","integration","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:14.532673214Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:18.302062857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:21.559483168Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:24.855352435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:27.478677090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:29.791234320Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":869,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Details: Integration Tests

### Test Strategy
Integration tests verify the auto-repair system works end-to-end with REAL
extension source code from the conformance corpus. They are the gatekeeper
before we graduate manual stubs (bd-k5q5.8.8).

### Test Structure
File: `tests/ext_auto_repair.rs` (new file, feature-gated with `ext-conformance`)

### Test Scenarios

**1. dist→src fallback (Pattern 1)**
- Remove the manual `dist/extension.js` stub from qualisero-pi-agent-scip
- Load the extension with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads successfully
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=DistToSrc is emitted
- Assert: the resolved path points to `src/extension.ts`

**2. Missing asset fallback (Pattern 2)**
- Remove the manual `form/` directory from nicobailon-interview-tool
- Load the extension with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads (readFileSync returns empty string)
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=MissingAsset is emitted for each file

**3. Monorepo escape (Pattern 3)**
- Remove the manual `shared/index.ts` stub
- Load qualisero-background-notify with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads successfully
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=MonorepoEscape is emitted
- Assert: the generated stub exports match what the extension imports

**4. npm proxy (Pattern 4)**
- Temporarily remove `@sourcegraph/scip-python` from virtual modules
- Load scip extension with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: extension loads, proxy stub provides no-op responses
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=NpmProxy is emitted

**5. Export shape normalization (Pattern 5)**
- Construct a test extension with CJS-style double-wrapped export
- Load with auto-repair enabled
- Assert: init() is found and called correctly
- Assert: RepairEvent with pattern=ExportShape is emitted

**6. All repairs disabled**
- Set all RepairConfig flags to false
- Load an extension that needs repair
- Assert: it FAILS (repairs don't fire when disabled)

**7. Repair event audit**
- Load multiple extensions that need different repairs
- Assert: repair_events() returns the correct count and patterns
- Assert: no false-positive repairs (extensions that load cleanly have no events)

**8. New extension validation**
- Download 2-3 extensions NOT in current corpus (from the 24 identified repos)
- Load them with auto-repair enabled
- Document which repairs fire (regression test for future)

### Test Infrastructure
- Uses the same `load_and_validate_extension()` from conformance_report.rs
- Adds `repair_events()` method to the test harness
- Feature-gated: only runs with `--features ext-conformance`
- Each test restores the original state (doesn't permanently remove stubs)

### Success Criteria
- All 8 test scenarios pass
- Repair events are accurate (no false positives)
- At least 1 NEW extension (not in corpus) loads via auto-repair","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:37:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","title":"Graduate manual stubs: remove hand-written fixes replaced by auto-repair","description":"## What

After auto-repair is implemented and integration-tested, remove the manual
stub files we created to get to 223/223.  The auto-repair pipeline should
handle these cases dynamically, so the stubs become dead code.

## Manual stubs to remove

1. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/shared/index.ts\`
   - Created for: qualisero-background-notify, qualisero-safe-git
   - Replaced by: Pattern 3 (monorepo escape stub generation)

2. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip/dist/extension.js\`
   - Created for: community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip
   - Replaced by: Pattern 1 (dist→src fallback)

3. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/nicobailon-interview-tool/form/\`
   - Created for: nicobailon-interview-tool
   - Replaced by: Pattern 2 (missing asset fallback)

4. \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/aliou-pi-processes/constants.ts\`
   \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/aliou-pi-processes/utils.ts\`
   \`tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/npm/aliou-pi-processes/components/processes-component.ts\`
   - Created for: aliou-pi-processes
   - Replaced by: Pattern 3 (monorepo escape) for most, Pattern 2 for some

## Process

1. For each stub, verify that the corresponding auto-repair pattern handles it
2. Remove the stub file
3. Run conformance_full_report to verify the extension still passes
4. Verify the repair event shows the correct pattern
5. Commit the removal

## Why NOT to remove all stubs at once

Some stubs may turn out to be better than auto-repair for specific cases.
For example, the shared/index.ts has carefully typed interfaces that the
auto-generated stub won't have.  If an extension's runtime behavior depends
on the stub having correct types/values, keep the manual stub and document why.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each removable stub is identified and tagged
- [ ] Each removal is verified with a conformance run
- [ ] Stubs that are better kept are documented with reasons
- [ ] No regression in conformance results

## Estimated Effort: 1-2 hours

## Dependencies
- Requires bd-k5q5.8.7 (integration tests passing)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:50.890583943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:37:28.870065180Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auto-repair","cleanup","extensions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:50.890583943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:52.831794974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":870,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.8.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Details: Graduate Manual Stubs

### What This Means
Once auto-repair is implemented and integration-tested, we can remove the
hand-written stubs that were created to achieve 100% conformance. The auto-repair
system should generate equivalent behavior dynamically.

### Manual Artifacts to Remove

**1. Filesystem Stubs (Pattern 1 & 2 replacements)**
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/qualisero-pi-agent-scip/dist/extension.js`
  → Auto-repair Pattern 1 handles this (dist→src fallback)
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/community/nicobailon-interview-tool/form/` (7 files)
  → Auto-repair Pattern 2 handles this (missing asset fallback)

**2. Monorepo Stubs (Pattern 3 replacement)**
- `tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/shared/index.ts` (17 exports)
  → Auto-repair Pattern 3 generates this dynamically from import analysis

**3. Virtual Module Stubs (Pattern 4 replacement - SELECTIVE)**
Some virtual modules should be KEPT because they provide nuanced behavior:
- KEEP: node:fs, node:path, node:os (system module shims with real behavior)
- KEEP: `@marckrenn/pi-sub-shared` (has specific MODEL_MULTIPLIERS data)
- REMOVE: Generic stubs that just export empty objects/functions
- Candidates for removal:
  - `@sourcegraph/scip-python` (proxy would work fine)
  - Simple no-op stubs that match the proxy pattern exactly

### Migration Process
For each stub removal:
1. Remove the stub (file or virtual module entry)
2. Run conformance tests with auto-repair enabled
3. Verify the extension still passes
4. Verify the RepairEvent matches the expected pattern
5. If test fails: investigate why and either improve auto-repair OR keep the stub

### Rollback Plan
- Keep all removed stubs in a git tag for easy rollback
- If auto-repair regresses, re-add the affected stubs
- Feature flags allow disabling individual patterns without code changes

### NOT in Scope
- Removing VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json fixes (those correct genuine expectation mismatches)
- Removing crypto_shim.rs (that provides real node:crypto functionality)
- Removing node:* module shims (those provide real behavior, not just stubs)

### Success Criteria
- At least 5 manual stubs removed and replaced by auto-repair
- Conformance still at 223/223 (or higher if new extensions added)
- No manual intervention needed for the graduated extensions
- Repair events logged for each auto-repaired extension","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:37:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9","title":"[LISR] Dynamic Secure Extension Repair Program (Intent-Legible Self-Healing)","description":"This program introduces a secure, deterministic, and auditable mechanism to dynamically repair broken extensions when intent is legible and risk can be bounded. Current extension breakage often comes from schema drift, API renames, and minor structural issues; we want a first-class self-healing path that reduces operator toil without compromising safety.

This epic exists to coordinate all required workstreams: policy and threat boundaries, legibility scoring, deterministic rewrites, constrained model assistance, behavior proofs, controlled rollout, and operational observability.","design":"Architecture is phase-gated and fail-closed:
1) Security contract and policy modes define what is allowed.
2) Intent legibility pipeline decides whether auto-repair is even eligible.
3) Deterministic rule engine performs known-safe rewrites first.
4) Optional model-assisted proposal stage is constrained by a primitive whitelist.
5) Proof pipeline verifies behavioral and capability invariants.
6) Overlay canary rollout validates production safety before promotion.
7) Full audit and telemetry enable forensic confidence and operator trust.","acceptance_criteria":"- End-to-end repair path exists from broken extension input to safe promoted output.
- Repair never increases privilege without explicit human approval.
- Validation/proof pipeline blocks unsafe or ambiguous repairs.
- Canary/rollback controls are automated and deterministic.
- Audit trail is complete enough for security review and incident forensics.","notes":"Strategic justification:
- Improves reliability and user trust in extension ecosystem.
- Reduces mean-time-to-recovery for breakages caused by upstream/API drift.
- Preserves project principle: safety and determinism over convenience.

Non-goals:
- No unconstrained AI patching.
- No silent privilege expansion.
- No executing untrusted broken code to infer intent.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:13.622771644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:25.741429171Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","program","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:30:13.622771644Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":833,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program context: this issue captures the complete self-healing strategy for extension repair when intent is legible and risk is bounded. Every child issue includes embedded rationale so future contributors do not need external planning documents.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:08Z"},{"id":845,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution map and dependency rationale:\n\nPhase A (Policy foundation): LISR-1 defines mode semantics, static-only boundaries, and privilege monotonicity. Every downstream epic depends on this because no repair behavior is acceptable without enforceable safety policy.\n\nPhase B (Eligibility and deterministic repair): LISR-2 determines whether intent is legible enough to repair. LISR-3 performs deterministic rewrites for known breakage classes. These two epics establish the safe default path and reduce reliance on model assistance.\n\nPhase C (Constrained AI path): LISR-4 is explicitly downstream of LISR-2 and LISR-3. We only allow model proposals after deterministic options and confidence gating are available, and only through strict primitive schemas.\n\nPhase D (Proof before activation): LISR-5 blocks production rollout. Structural checks, capability monotonicity, hostcall parity, and conformance replay/golden checksums are the hard activation gate.\n\nPhase E (Runtime rollout safety): LISR-6 adds overlay artifacts, canary routing, and deterministic rollback. This assumes proof pipeline outputs are trustworthy and machine-readable.\n\nPhase F (Operational trust): LISR-7 captures append-only ledger, telemetry, operator inspection, and forensic bundles to support incident response and governance.\n\nPhase G (Institutional memory): LISR-8 codifies ADRs, playbooks, developer guidance, and release governance so system intent survives team turnover.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:43Z"},{"id":856,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Detailed phase rationale (human-readable version):

Phase A - Policy Foundation:
LISR-1 is upstream of all work because no repair automation is acceptable without mode semantics, static-only boundaries, and privilege monotonicity controls.

Phase B - Legibility + Deterministic Repair:
LISR-2 determines eligibility (is intent legible enough?) and LISR-3 provides deterministic, auditable repair behavior for common breakage classes.

Phase C - Constrained AI Assist:
LISR-4 is intentionally downstream of LISR-2 and LISR-3 so model usage remains bounded, policy-governed, and secondary to deterministic fixes.

Phase D - Proof Gate:
LISR-5 is the hard activation barrier. Candidates must pass structural checks, security invariants, semantic parity checks, and conformance replay.

Phase E - Runtime Safety:
LISR-6 delivers canary and rollback controls to handle real-world runtime regressions even after offline proof passes.

Phase F - Trust and Operations:
LISR-7 ensures every decision is observable, explainable, and reconstructable for incidents.

Phase G - Long-Term Maintainability:
LISR-8 captures rationale, operator procedures, and governance so future teams can evolve the system safely.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:25Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","title":"[LISR-1] Security Contract and Repair Policy Framework","description":"Define non-negotiable safety constraints, policy modes, and privilege boundaries that govern all repair behavior.","design":"Codify mode semantics (off/suggest/auto-safe/auto-strict), static-only constraints, privilege monotonicity, and high-risk approval requirements as machine-enforced rules.","acceptance_criteria":"Policy engine enforces fail-closed behavior, mode semantics are test-covered, and all downstream repair flows consume the same authoritative policy decisions.","notes":"This is the foundational trust layer. If this is weak, the entire self-healing system is unsafe regardless of model quality or rewrite correctness.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:23.580113284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:27.620415186Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","policy","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:23.580113284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":834,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Security-first ordering is intentional: no repair implementation should proceed without policy and threat constraints being machine-enforced.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:08Z"},{"id":846,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency intent for LISR-1:\n- 1.1 is the control-plane entry point (mode resolution).\n- 1.2, 1.3, and 1.4 all depend on 1.1 because behavior must be mode-aware and deterministic.\n- This epic is upstream of all other LISR epics by design; it defines what is allowed before discussing what is possible.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:43Z"},{"id":857,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LISR-1 sequencing note:
- 1.1 defines authoritative mode resolution.
- 1.2/1.3/1.4 consume that mode context.
This enforces one control plane for safety posture.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:27Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","title":"[LISR-1.1] Implement repair policy modes and config/CLI resolution","description":"Implement unified configuration for repair modes (off, suggest, auto-safe, auto-strict) with deterministic precedence across defaults, config, env, and CLI.","design":"Extend config schema and CLI plumbing so mode resolution is transparent and testable; include explicit diagnostics for active mode and source.","acceptance_criteria":"Mode resolution is deterministic, documented, and fully covered by precedence tests; invalid values fail fast with actionable errors.","notes":"This prevents hidden behavior drift and ensures operators always know which risk posture is active.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:29.978592666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:59.866720630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","extensions","lisr","policy","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:29.978592666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":842,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: mode names were normalized after shell interpolation noise; canonical policy modes are off/suggest/auto-safe/auto-strict.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:59Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.2","title":"[LISR-1.2] Enforce static-only pre-repair safety boundary","description":"Guarantee broken extension artifacts are never executed during intent extraction or repair planning.","design":"Add static-analysis-only guardrails around the pipeline and reject flows that attempt runtime execution prior to validation gates.","acceptance_criteria":"Any code path that would execute extension logic before proof gates is blocked and tested; violations emit high-signal security diagnostics.","notes":"This removes an entire class of exploit vectors from the repair process.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:30.628244470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:58.571287545Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","policy","repair","sandbox"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:30.628244470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:58.571215531Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","title":"[LISR-1.3] Implement privilege monotonicity checker","description":"Implement a formal checker that ensures repaired artifacts do not broaden effective capabilities/scopes relative to original policy state.","design":"Compare capability sets and scoped grants before and after repair, with explicit handling for method families (exec, env, http, fs, tool, session, ui, events, log).","acceptance_criteria":"Checker emits machine-readable diffs and blocks non-approved privilege expansion.","notes":"Monotonicity is the core safety invariant for autonomous repair.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:31.729193774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:00.092597260Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capabilities","extensions","lisr","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:31.729193774Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:58.847963007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":843,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: method-family list was repaired to canonical values after command-substitution noise during bulk import.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.4","title":"[LISR-1.4] Add high-risk surface approval gates","description":"Require explicit approval for any repair touching high-risk capability surfaces or sensitive hostcall patterns.","design":"Define high-risk classes and wire fail-closed prompts/workflow for escalation paths in strict and safe modes.","acceptance_criteria":"Unapproved high-risk changes are denied; approvals are logged with actor, rationale, and timestamp.","notes":"This ensures human control where automation risk is inherently higher.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:32.427635685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:59.123737360Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["approval","extensions","lisr","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:32.427635685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:59.123689210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","title":"[LISR-1] Security Contract and Repair Policy Framework","description":"Define non-negotiable safety constraints, policy modes, and privilege boundaries that govern all repair behavior.","design":"Codify mode semantics (off/suggest/auto-safe/auto-strict), static-only constraints, privilege monotonicity, and high-risk approval requirements as machine-enforced rules.","acceptance_criteria":"Policy engine enforces fail-closed behavior, mode semantics are test-covered, and all downstream repair flows consume the same authoritative policy decisions.","notes":"This is the foundational trust layer. If this is weak, the entire self-healing system is unsafe regardless of model quality or rewrite correctness.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:23.580113284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:27:18.168532683Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:27:18.168444128Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","policy","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:23.580113284Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":834,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Security-first ordering is intentional: no repair implementation should proceed without policy and threat constraints being machine-enforced.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:08Z"},{"id":846,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency intent for LISR-1:\n- 1.1 is the control-plane entry point (mode resolution).\n- 1.2, 1.3, and 1.4 all depend on 1.1 because behavior must be mode-aware and deterministic.\n- This epic is upstream of all other LISR epics by design; it defines what is allowed before discussing what is possible.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:43Z"},{"id":857,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LISR-1 sequencing note:
- 1.1 defines authoritative mode resolution.
- 1.2/1.3/1.4 consume that mode context.
This enforces one control plane for safety posture.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:27Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","title":"[LISR-1.1] Implement repair policy modes and config/CLI resolution","description":"Implement unified configuration for repair modes (off, suggest, auto-safe, auto-strict) with deterministic precedence across defaults, config, env, and CLI.","design":"Extend config schema and CLI plumbing so mode resolution is transparent and testable; include explicit diagnostics for active mode and source.","acceptance_criteria":"Mode resolution is deterministic, documented, and fully covered by precedence tests; invalid values fail fast with actionable errors.","notes":"This prevents hidden behavior drift and ensures operators always know which risk posture is active.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:29.978592666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:00:00.732709686Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:00:00.732618356Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","extensions","lisr","policy","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:29.978592666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":842,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: mode names were normalized after shell interpolation noise; canonical policy modes are off/suggest/auto-safe/auto-strict.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:59Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.2","title":"[LISR-1.2] Enforce static-only pre-repair safety boundary","description":"Guarantee broken extension artifacts are never executed during intent extraction or repair planning.","design":"Add static-analysis-only guardrails around the pipeline and reject flows that attempt runtime execution prior to validation gates.","acceptance_criteria":"Any code path that would execute extension logic before proof gates is blocked and tested; violations emit high-signal security diagnostics.","notes":"This removes an entire class of exploit vectors from the repair process.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:30.628244470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:15:21.457527864Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:15:21.457436734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","lisr","policy","repair","sandbox"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:30.628244470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:58.571215531Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","title":"[LISR-1.3] Implement privilege monotonicity checker","description":"Implement a formal checker that ensures repaired artifacts do not broaden effective capabilities/scopes relative to original policy state.","design":"Compare capability sets and scoped grants before and after repair, with explicit handling for method families (exec, env, http, fs, tool, session, ui, events, log).","acceptance_criteria":"Checker emits machine-readable diffs and blocks non-approved privilege expansion.","notes":"Monotonicity is the core safety invariant for autonomous repair.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:31.729193774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:23:26.142426935Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:23:26.142337609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capabilities","extensions","lisr","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:31.729193774Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:58.847963007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":843,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: method-family list was repaired to canonical values after command-substitution noise during bulk import.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:00Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.4","title":"[LISR-1.4] Add high-risk surface approval gates","description":"Require explicit approval for any repair touching high-risk capability surfaces or sensitive hostcall patterns.","design":"Define high-risk classes and wire fail-closed prompts/workflow for escalation paths in strict and safe modes.","acceptance_criteria":"Unapproved high-risk changes are denied; approvals are logged with actor, rationale, and timestamp.","notes":"This ensures human control where automation risk is inherently higher.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:32.427635685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:26:56.708065095Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T22:26:56.707947726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["approval","extensions","lisr","repair","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:32.427635685Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:59.123689210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","title":"[LISR-2] Intent Legibility Analysis and Confidence Gating","description":"Determine whether broken extensions are sufficiently legible for safe automated repair.","design":"Build deterministic intent extraction from manifest/hooks/hostcalls/AST signals, then compute confidence with explainable reason codes.","acceptance_criteria":"Legibility gate can distinguish safe-to-repair vs ambiguous cases, emits explanation payloads, and is integrated as a hard precondition to any automatic repair.","notes":"Legibility gating prevents guessy repair behavior and keeps automation constrained to high-confidence scenarios.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:24.466151334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:27.920751408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","extensions","intent","lisr","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:24.466151334Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:53.630704930Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":835,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Legibility gating is the anti-hallucination control. If intent cannot be explained deterministically, automation must stop or downgrade to suggestion-only output.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:33:09Z"},{"id":847,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency intent for LISR-2:\n- 2.1 and 2.2 build objective evidence (intent graph + ambiguity detection).\n- 2.3 computes confidence from those signals.\n- 2.4 provides the canonical gate contract consumed by repair orchestration.\nThis epic exists to prevent unsafe guessing; if confidence is low, automation must degrade safely.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:34:43Z"},{"id":858,"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LISR-2 sequencing note:
- Extract objective intent evidence.
- Detect ambiguity.
- Convert evidence to confidence and reason codes.
- Publish a deterministic gate contract.","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:35:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9.2.1","title":"[LISR-2.1] Build intent graph extractor from manifest/hooks/hostcalls","description":"Extract normalized intent graph from extension manifest declarations, registered hooks/tools/providers, and hostcall usage surfaces.","design":"Create reusable intermediate representation that downstream scoring and validation logic can consume consistently.","acceptance_criteria":"Extractor handles partial/broken inputs with graceful degradation and structured diagnostics.","notes":"A stable IR is required for explainable, testable legibility decisions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:33.133536141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:33.481836637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","extensions","intent","lisr","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:33.133536141Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-k5q5.9.2.2","title":"[LISR-2.2] Implement tolerant AST recovery and ambiguity detection","description":"Add tolerant parsing/recovery for partially broken extensions and detect ambiguous constructs that reduce repair confidence.","design":"Track ambiguity classes (syntax fracture, unresolved symbol clusters, conflicting hook signatures) as first-class scoring inputs.","acceptance_criteria":"Recovery pipeline produces deterministic ambiguity metadata for identical inputs.","notes":"Without ambiguity detection, confidence scores are over-optimistic and unsafe.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:33.803918588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:34.149689110Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ast","extensions","intent","lisr","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-k5q5.9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:32:33.803918588Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
